description: Per-collection dump/download with streaming BSON export, progress bar, and cancellation support

userStories.title: Add exportCollection method to MongoService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a streaming BSON export method so the main process can dump a collection to any writable stream with constant memory
acceptanceCriteria: exportCollection(dbName, collName, output, onProgress, signal) method exists on MongoService
Uses collection.find({}, { raw: true }) for zero-deserialize BSON buffers (fallback to BSON.serialize if typing issues)
Iterates cursor with for-await, writes each buffer to output stream
Handles backpressure via drain event
Checks signal.aborted each iteration, closes cursor and returns error if aborted
Throttles onProgress callback by time (~200ms) not doc count
Returns Result<number> with exported doc count on success
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: raw: true on find returns Buffer directly from driver — no JS deserialization overhead. If TypeScript types fight us, fallback to BSON.serialize(doc) per document.

userStories.title: Add export and cancel IPC handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IPC handlers that show a save dialog, stream the export to a gzipped file, send progress events, and support cancellation
acceptanceCriteria: mongo:export-collection handler registered in ipc-handlers.ts
Shows dialog.showSaveDialog with default filename {collName}.bson.gz and filter ['bson.gz']
Returns { ok: true, data: null } if dialog cancelled
Rejects with error if export already in progress for that collection (Map<string, AbortController>)
Creates gzip stream piped to file write stream
Sends progress to renderer via event.sender.send('export:progress', { db, collection, count })
On complete: removes from active map, returns { ok: true, data: count }
On abort/error: destroys gzip and file streams, fs.unlink partial file, removes from map
mongo:cancel-export handler looks up AbortController and calls abort()
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Does not use wrap() helper — needs dialog + stream + progress lifecycle. Import dialog and BrowserWindow from electron, createWriteStream from fs, createGzip from zlib.

userStories.title: Expose export API in preload bridge and type definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the export, cancel, and progress APIs exposed to the renderer process with correct types
acceptanceCriteria: exportCollection(db, collection) added to preload api object
cancelExport(db, collection) added to preload api object
onExportProgress(cb) added — strips Electron IpcRendererEvent before calling cb, returns cleanup function
MongoApi interface in index.d.ts updated with all three methods
Missing loadHistory, saveHistory, clearHistory added to index.d.ts to fix existing TS errors
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: onExportProgress must wrap the ipcRenderer.on handler to strip the Electron event: (_event, data) => cb(data)

userStories.title: Add download icon and progress bar to Sidebar collection rows
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a download icon on hover over each collection, a progress bar background fill during export, and an X icon to cancel
acceptanceCriteria: Download icon (lucide-react) appears inline right of collection name on hover
Icon uses opacity-0 group-hover/coll:opacity-100 pattern
Clicking download icon does not trigger collection selection (stopPropagation)
Clicking download icon opens save dialog and starts export
During export: icon changes to X, always visible (not hover-only)
During export: row background fills left-to-right using linear-gradient with accent color based on count/coll.count
Clicking X cancels export and clears progress state
Toast shown on export complete with doc count
Toast shown on export error
Double-click safe: cannot start second export on same collection
Progress state is local useState, not in store
useEffect subscribes to onExportProgress, cleans up on unmount
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Progress % = exportedCount / coll.count (estimatedDocumentCount). Not exact but good enough for a progress bar.
